title: Dragon spear
wikitext:
{{External|rs}} {{Infobox Item |version1 = Unpoisoned |version2 = Poison |version3 = Poison+ |version4 = Poison++ |version5 = Karambwan poison |bucketname1 = (unp) |bucketname2 = (p) |bucketname3 = (p+) |bucketname4 = (p++) |bucketname5 = (kp) |name1 = Dragon spear |name2 = Dragon spear(p) |name3 = Dragon spear(p+) |name4 = Dragon spear(p++) |name5 = Dragon spear(kp) |image1 = [[File:Dragon spear.png]] |image2 = [[File:Dragon spear(p).png]] |image3 = [[File:Dragon spear(p+).png]] |image4 = [[File:Dragon spear(p++).png]] |image5 = [[File:Dragon spear(kp).png]] |release1 = [[29 March]] [[2004]] |release2 = [[29 March]] [[2004]] |release3 = [[11 July]] [[2005]] |release4 = [[11 July]] [[2005]] |release5 = [[14 September]] [[2004]] |update1 = RS2 Launched! |update2 = RS2 Launched! |update3 = Farming |update4 = Farming |update5 = Tai Bwo Wannai Trio |members = Yes |quest = No |tradeable1 = Yes |tradeable2 = Yes |tradeable3 = Yes |tradeable4 = Yes |tradeable5 = No |placeholder = Yes |equipable = Yes |stackable = No |noteable1 = Yes |noteable2 = Yes |noteable3 = Yes |noteable4 = Yes |noteable5 = No |options = Wield, Drop |examine1 = A dragon tipped spear. |examine2 = A poisoned dragon tipped spear. |examine3 = A poisoned dragon tipped spear. |examine4 = A poisoned dragon tipped spear. |examine5 = A Karambwan poisoned dragon tipped spear. |value = 62400 |weight = 2.267 |exchange1 = Yes |exchange2 = Yes |exchange3 = Yes |exchange4 = Yes |exchange5 = No |id1 = 1249 |id2 = 1263 |id3 = 5716 |id4 = 5730 |id5 = 3176 |leagueRegion = General }} {{Synced switch |version1 = [[File:Dragon spear detail.png|left|100px]] |version2 = [[File:Dragon spear(p) detail.png|left|100px]] |version3 = [[File:Dragon spear(p+) detail.png|left|100px]] |version4 = [[File:Dragon spear(p++) detail.png|left|100px]] |version5 = [[File:Dragon spear(kp) detail.png|left|100px]] }} The '''dragon spear''' is a two-handed [[melee weapon]] that requires level 60 [[Attack]] to wield. It does not require the completion of a [[quest]] to equip. Like most [[spear]]s, it can be [[Poison#Weapon poisoning|poisoned]]. The spear is a rare drop from both the [[rare drop table|rare]] and [[gem drop table]]s. On the ''gem'' drop table, the spear will only drop if the [[Legends' Quest]] has been completed, otherwise a talisman will be dropped in its place. However, the ''rare'' drop table does not require the quest's completion in order to be able to receive it as a drop. Wearing a [[ring of wealth]] will increase the chances of receiving one from both tables. [[Gorak]]s fought in the [[Gorak Plane]] while wearing a ring of wealth are more likely to drop the dragon spear than any other monster, with a drop rate of 1 in 325. [[Brutal black dragon]]s are also frequent droppers of dragon spears, dropping it directly with a drop rate of 1 in 512 (while also having access to the rare drop table so they drop it slightly more common than this overall). The spear is primarily used for [[player killing]] scenarios; outside of this, it is sought after by ironmen, as two of these are needed for elite clue scrolls. As with most [[dragon equipment]], the dragon spear '''cannot''' be made from raw materials using the [[Smithing]] skill. ==Combat stats== {{Infobox Bonuses |version1 = Unpoisoned |version2 = Poison |version3 = Poison+ |version4 = Poison++ |version5 = Karambwan poison |bucketname1 = (unp) |bucketname2 = (p) |bucketname3 = (p+) |bucketname4 = (p++) |bucketname5 = (kp) |astab = +55 |aslash = +55 |acrush = +55 |amagic = 0 |arange = 0 |dstab = +5 |dslash = +5 |dcrush = +5 |dmagic = +5 |drange = +5 |str = +60 |rstr = 0 |mdmg = 0 |prayer = 0 |slot = 2h |speed = 4 |attackrange = 1 |combatstyle = Spear |image1 = [[File:Dragon spear equipped male.png]] |image2 = [[File:Dragon spear(p) equipped male.png]] |image3 = [[File:Dragon spear(p+) equipped male.png]] |image4 = [[File:Dragon spear(p++) equipped male.png]] |image5 = [[File:Dragon spear(kp) equipped male.png]] |altimage1 = [[File:Dragon spear equipped female.png]] |altimage2 = [[File:Dragon spear(p) equipped female.png]] |altimage3 = [[File:Dragon spear(p+) equipped female.png]] |altimage4 = [[File:Dragon spear(p++) equipped female.png]] |altimage5 = [[File:Dragon spear(kp) equipped female.png]] }} {{CombatStyles|Spear|speed=4|attackrange=1}} ==Special attack== [[File:Shove (dragon spear).gif|thumb|250px|A player performs the dragon spear's special attack, ''Shove''.]] The dragon spear has a [[special attacks|special attack]], ''Shove'', which it shares with the [[Zamorakian spear]] and [[Zamorakian hasta|hasta]]. It pushes the target back and [[Stun (status)|stuns]] them for 5 ticks (3.0 seconds), consuming 25% of the player's special attack energy. ''Shove'' has an attack speed of 5 ticks compared to the spear's normal attack speed of 4 ticks (2.4 seconds). The effects of ''Shove'' do not stack, meaning that players cannot use it on a target who is already stunned. Stunned players are also given a one tick (0.6 seconds) period of immunity after a stun wears off during which they cannot be stunned again. This effectively means that ''Shove'' cannot be used on the same player more than once every 6 ticks. In addition to this, as soon as the stun wears off, the stunned player is granted a 5 tick (3.0 seconds) period of immunity against being bound by binding spells like [[Entangle]] or [[Ice Barrage]]. Despite this, ''Shove'' is popular in player killing scenarios as the brief stun causes all incoming damage to be stalled until it expires, after which all damage taken is applied at once. Under certain conditions, ''Shove'' can push the target diagonally past obstacles. This only occurs when pushing a target east or west, and if there is a free tile directly north or south of the obstacle — e.g. 1x1 obstacles, the corner of larger ones, or diagonal walls. Targets will not be pushed past obstacles when pushing to the north or south. ''Shove'' displays a preference for certain tiles; when pushing a target east or west past a 1x1 obstacle, the north or south free tile will always be chosen, respectively. On non-Castle Wars worlds, players within Castle Wars are able to shove targets back-to-back using this special attack.{{PlainCiteNews|url=https://secure.runescape.com/m=news/poll-83-castle-wars-update?oldschool=1|title=Poll 83: Castle Wars Update|newsdate=2 April 2025}} ''Shove'' cannot be used against large monsters, such as [[giants]], because they are too big to push back. If a player tries this, the special attack does not occur and no special attack energy is consumed. A message appears in the chat box, reading, "That creature is too large to knock back!" A general way to tell if the spear can use the "Shove" special attack is to determine how much space the target monster takes up. If it takes up more than one game tile, it cannot be shoved. For example, a player can shove an [[ice warrior]], which takes up one game tile, but not an [[ice giant]], which takes up four. ==Creation== {{Switch infobox |text1 = Poison |item1 = {{Recipe |members = Yes |ticks = 0 |mat1 = Dragon spear |mat1quantity = 1 |mat2 = Weapon poison |mat2quantity = 1 |output1 = Dragon spear(p) |output1quantity = 1 }} |text2 = Poison+ |item2 = {{Recipe |members = Yes |ticks = 0 |mat1 = Dragon spear |mat1quantity = 1 |mat2 = Weapon poison(+) |mat2quantity = 1 |output1 = Dragon spear(p+) |output1quantity = 1 }} |text3 = Poison++ |item3 = {{Recipe |members = Yes |ticks = 0 |mat1 = Dragon spear |mat1quantity = 1 |mat2 = Weapon poison(++) |mat2quantity = 1 |output1 = Dragon spear(p++) |output1quantity = 1 }} |text4 = Karambwan poison |item4 = {{Recipe |members = Yes |ticks = 0 |mat1 = Dragon spear |mat1quantity = 1 |mat2 = Karambwan paste (poison) |mat2txt = Karambwan paste |mat2quantity = 1 |mat2cost = No |output1 = Dragon spear(kp) |output1quantity = 1 |output1cost = No }} }} ==Item sources== {{Drop sources|Dragon spear}} In addition to the sources in the above table, a Dragon spear can be obtained as a drop from the [[Rare drop table]], accessible by most Slayer monsters. [[Goraks]] also drop Dragon spears at a rate of 1/325 with a [[ring of wealth]] equipped. ==Products== {{Uses material list|Dragon spear}} ==Treasure Trails== {{EmoteClueHeader}} {{EmoteClue|tier=elite|emote=Panic|location=[[Trollweiss]] flowers after [[sled]]ding down [[Trollweiss Mountain]]|item1=Dragon spear|item2=blue d'hide vambraces|item3=rune plateskirt}} {{EmoteClue|tier=elite|emote=Spin|location=Inside [[West Ardougne]]'s church|item1=Dragon spear|item2=red d'hide chaps}} {{EmoteClueBottom}} A poisoned dragon spear of any tier will '''NOT''' work for these steps. ==Used in recommended equipment== {{Used in recommended equipment|Dragon spear}} ==Changes== {{Subject changes header}} {{Subject changes |date = 21 April 2021 |update = Fishing Trawler Improvements and Equipment Rebalance: Part One |change = The spear's [[attack speed]] has been increased from 5 (every 3.0 seconds) to 4 (every 2.4 seconds). }} {{Subject changes |date = 28 July 2016 |update = Dragon Spear Changes & Beta 3 |change = You can no longer use the Dragon spear, Zamorakian spear or Zamorakian hasta special attack on a target who is already stunned. In addition to this, players are given a 1 tick period of immunity after a stun wears off in which they cannot be stunned again. }} {{Subject changes |date = 22 August 2006 |update = Naming conventions |change = *The extra strong poisoned variant was renamed from "Dragon spear(+)" to "Dragon spear(p+)". *The super strong poisoned variant was renamed from "Dragon spear(s)" to "Dragon spear(p++)". }} {{Subject changes |date = 9 May 2005 |update = Barrows |change = The spear's attack speed was increased to 5 ticks (3.0 seconds). }} {{Subject changes |date = 14 June 2004 |update = Special Attacks |change = The item was given a special attack, ''Shove''. }} {{Subject changes |daterange = 20 July –
7 September 2004 |change = The poisoned version's examine was changed from "''A dragon tipped spear.''" to "''A poisoned dragon tipped spear.''" }} {{Subject changes |date = 29 March 2004 |update = RS2 Launched! |change = The item became permanently available with the launch of ''RuneScape 2''. }} {{Subject changes |date = |change = The item was added to the ''RuneScape 2'' Beta. }} {{Subject changes footer}} ==Trivia== *The dragon spear's special attack was changed on [[28 July]] [[2016]], due to many player complaints on how it was considered overpowered. Prior to the fix, large [[Wilderness]] [[clans]] could use the spear's special to "stunlock" the victim. The victim would be attacked by a group of players using [[Smite]], while another group would continually spam the special attack on the victim. The victim would be unable to perform any action, since they were being [[Stun (status)|stunned]] and the game did not register the damage. Once the stuns stopped, the player would take all the damage they received while being stunned in one game tick, which would also drain them of all of their prayer. The only way for players to escape this was by x-logging, as the game would take them out of the server after a minute passed. *[[Tim]]'s spear is strikingly similar to a dragon spear. ==References== {{reflist}} {{Stab weapons}} {{Dragon equipment}} [[Category:Weapons with Special attacks]] [[Category:Collection log items]] [[Category:Weapons that inflict poison]]
